mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE monthly change exceeds 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where unemployment rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand contraction that depresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force rapid equity repricing and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns during post-shock recovery periods, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting aggressive equity positioning that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5 and FEDFUNDS remains below 1.5
failure_condition: The specified inflation-rate regime produces negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day S&P 500 returns across multiple rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer momentum relative to output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP-retail sales divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with an information coefficient greater than 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals capacity utilization peaks that precede central bank rate hikes, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration during tightening cycles yields positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in >60% of observed cases.
